feed.title: Combined summary - [BIP Proposal] Removal of BIP 125 RBF signalling in wallet transactions

entry.title: Combined summary - [BIP Proposal] Removal of BIP 125 RBF signalling in wallet transactions
entry.summary: The recent discussions on the Bitcoin Core mailing list have centered around the proposal to remove BIP 125 Replace-By-Fee (RBF) signaling from transactions. Since the introduction of full RBF in Bitcoin Core v28.0, the need for explicit signaling has been deemed redundant. This change reflects the evolving practices within the Bitcoin network regarding transaction management and replaceability. A pertinent issue raised in this context relates to the default input sequence number used in transactions. There is a consensus among developers that it should align with the best practices agreed upon by the broader wallet community. The discussion emphasizes the potential for an informational BIP that would recommend a specific input sequence number, fostering uniformity across various wallet implementations. Murch, another participant in the discussion, provided insights into the practical implications of this change. He highlighted that while removing signaling might simplify transaction processes, the choice of per-input sequence values remains critical. Currently, the most common sequence settings are final (MAX), non-final (MAX−1), and non-final replaceable (MAX−2), with the latter being predominantly used as per data indicating about 75% of transactions currently signal replaceability based on a 30-day average from [Mainnet Observer](https://mainnet.observer/charts/transactions-signaling-explicit-rbf/). Given the network’s adoption of mempoolfullrbf, where transactions are considered replaceable by default, Murch suggests a convergence towards using (MAX−2) for input sequences. This approach would reflect the network's behavior more accurately and mitigate confusion around the intent signaled by other less common sequence values. Such a shift requires careful consideration and collaboration among wallet developers to ensure a smooth transition and maintain network integrity and predictability in transaction handling.
